summary.intent: Complete a PR review cycle on PR #662 in fogodev/ars-ui by addressing Codex review comments, resolving threads, and maintaining CI green.
summary.outcome: Both Codex review threads were replied to and resolved, a fresh Codex review pass was requested, and a formatting fix (wrapping a long test line) was committed and pushed to keep CI passing.

repo: The repo uses a 'snapshot-reviewed' label that must be re-applied after any snapshot changes in a push.
Codex review status is tracked via GitHub issue reactions (e.g., 'eyes' reaction indicates review in progress).
CI enforces strict cargo fmt formatting checks — even minor long-line violations will fail the build.

code.finding: A test line was too long and required cargo fmt wrapping to pass CI formatting checks.

workflow: After pushing snapshot changes, the workflow requires: re-apply label, reply to review threads, resolve threads, then request a new Codex review pass — in that order.
Running cargo fmt before pushing is necessary to avoid CI failures on formatting checks.
Codex review round-trip takes at least 60+ seconds; polling with sleep is the current approach to track completion.

friction: CI caught a formatting issue only after push, requiring an extra commit/push cycle that could have been caught locally first.
The Codex review re-request workflow is manual and multi-step (label + reply + resolve + comment), with no single command to orchestrate it.
Polling for Codex review completion relies on sleep + reaction checks rather than a webhook or event-driven mechanism.
open_items: Second Codex review pass was still in progress at the end of the session — its findings are unresolved.
CI checks had restarted but were not fully complete when the session ended; final CI status is unknown.
